# Caddyfile.d — app route fragments

This directory is **owned by `outpost onboard`**, not by humans. Each onboarded
application drops one `<app>.caddy` file here; Caddy imports them via the
`import /etc/caddy/Caddyfile.d/*.caddy` directive in the main `Caddyfile`.

**Do not commit per-app fragments to the infras repo.** The infras repo
remains generic; per-app routing knowledge lives in the application's own
repository (in its `outpost.app.yaml`) and is rendered into this directory
at onboard time.

## What a fragment looks like

```caddy
# core/compose/Caddyfile.d/myapp.caddy — generated by `outpost onboard myapp`
@myapp host {$MYAPP_HOST:myapp}.{$ROOT_DOMAIN}
handle @myapp {
    handle /api/* {
        reverse_proxy myapp:8081
    }
    handle {
        reverse_proxy myapp:8080
    }
}
```

Fragments use the same Caddy DSL as the main Caddyfile and inherit the
top-level `{ auto_https off; admin off; ... }` block automatically. They
must not declare a new `:80 { ... }` site block.

## Reloading

After dropping or editing a fragment, Caddy needs to re-read its config:

```bash
docker exec caddy caddy reload --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
```

`outpost onboard` does this automatically.

## Reference / examples

See `docs/onboarding/example-fragments/` for fully worked examples
(multi-product routing, path rewrites, dashboard split, etc.).
